scope: License-safe visual and audio direction for Ancient Memphis: A Walk Through the White Walls, focused on early Ptah / Hwt-ka-Ptah precinct and Kom el-Fakhry / Mit Rahina settlement evidence.
policy: Use CC0, public-domain, owned, commissioned, or explicitly permitted source media for dataset/fine-tuning experiments.
Use restricted site-specific pages only as human research references and evidence notes.
Runtime assets in this pass are generated procedurally by the project and do not copy source media pixels, scans, photos, video frames, or audio.

approvedReferenceSources.role: Broad Egyptian object, relief, clothing, tool, animal, and material-culture visual study.
Old Kingdom relief color, line rhythm, figures, clothing, animals, and agricultural ambience.
CC0 Egyptian collection references for objects, relief fragments, and material details.
CC0 2D/3D object references where Egyptian material is relevant.
Broad geospatial and landscape orientation only.
Memphis and Mit Rahina landscape context; not media training input.
Kom el-Fakhry, Mit Rahina, Ptah precinct, and archaeological framing; not media training input.
Kom el-Fakhry project context; not media training input.
Hwt-ka-Ptah / Great Temple of Memphis identifier; not image training input.
Official Ptah precinct context; not media training input.
Future text/language tooling for verified inscriptions and transliteration, not speech audio in this pass.

runtimeAssetTargets.description: Sun-baked mudbrick, plaster dust, mortar seams, and surface roughness for houses and low walls.
White Walls threshold, Ptah precinct exterior, cut limestone, and worn plaster.
Decorative painted shrine texture inspired by Old Kingdom visual language, without copying source media or claiming exact Memphis iconography.
Nile-side water, reeds, boats, mooring posts, baskets, and cloth surfaces.
Synthetic Nile, birds, market, footsteps, and temple ambience; no copied recordings and no claimed authentic ancient speech.
